verses.text: Israel came to the wilderness of Zin; they abode in Kadesh. Miriam died there, was buried there. Guidance: One line, and a prophetess is gone. Miriam — who watched her baby brother in the bulrushes, who led the women in song after the Red Sea — buried in the wilderness her brothers will also die in. The text doesn't slow down. But the camp pauses; her grave marks the beginning of the end of the wilderness generation.

There was no water for the congregation. They gathered against Moses and Aaron: would God we had died when our brethren died before the LORD! Wherefore have ye brought up the congregation into this evil place? — no figs, vines, pomegranates, no water. Guidance: A new generation, same complaint. Forty years on, the script hasn't changed. Death wished, blame placed, Egypt's fruit suddenly remembered with longing. Thirst exposes the heart — what was buried in the parents resurfaces in the children.

Moses and Aaron went to the door of the tabernacle, fell on their faces. The glory of the LORD appeared. The LORD: take the rod, gather the assembly, SPEAK ye unto the rock — it shall give forth water. Moses took the rod. Guidance: God's instructions are precise. Take the rod, but use the voice. The rod is for authority; the word is for the miracle. Forty years ago He told Moses to strike a rock. Today He says speak.

The LORD: take the rod, gather the assembly, SPEAK ye unto the rock — it shall give forth water. Moses took the rod. Guidance: God's instructions are precise. Take the rod, but use the voice. The rod is for authority; the word is for the miracle. Forty years ago He told Moses to strike a rock. Today He says speak.

Moses gathered the congregation before the rock: hear now, ye rebels — must we fetch you water out of this rock? Moses lifted up his hand, and smote the rock TWICE with his rod. Water came out abundantly; the congregation drank, and their beasts. Guidance: Forty years of patience cracked in one moment. 'We' fetch you water — Moses takes credit; the rebels' word lands in his own mouth. He hits what God said to address. The water still comes — God's mercy outruns His servant's failure.

The LORD to Moses and Aaron: because ye believed Me not, to sanctify Me before Israel, ye shall not bring this congregation into the land. This is the water of Meribah — Israel strove with the LORD, and He was sanctified in them. Guidance: One angry strike costs Moses the promised land. Leaders are judged at a higher altitude. The same God who used him forty years bars him from the river. Holy is what God IS — we mustn't smear that in His name.

Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom: thou knowest our travail. Let us pass by the king's highway — we will not turn aside, not drink of the well; if we drink, we will pay. Edom: thou shalt not pass, lest I come out with the sword. Israel turned away. Guidance: Edom is Jacob's brother — Esau's line. Family closes the door. Israel does not force the passage; sometimes faith takes the longer road around. God can lead even when relatives won't.

Israel came to Mount Hor. The LORD: Aaron shall be gathered to his people, not enter the land, because ye rebelled at Meribah. Strip Aaron of his garments, put them upon Eleazar his son. Moses did so. Aaron died on the top of the mount. Israel mourned thirty days. Guidance: Aaron undresses on a mountain so his son can put on the robes. The priesthood does not pause at the grave. A holy office in a quiet ceremony — and then thirty days of tears.
